ucs.title: [LLM] Cadet Blizzard / Ember Bear WhisperGate stager — Discord CDN payload pull followed by raw PhysicalDrive write
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical WhisperGate kill chain attributed to Ember Bear (Cadet Blizzard / UAC-0056 / DEV-0586 / GRU Unit 29155): a non-browser process retrieves a stage payload from cdn.discordapp.com/attachments, drops to %TEMP%/%APPDATA%, and the resulting binary opens a handle to \\.\PhysicalDrive0 to overwrite the MBR. Both halves are characteristic of the January 2022 Ukraine wiper campaign and follow-on Unit 29155 operations.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest IN ("cdn.discordapp.com","media.discordapp.net") AND All_Traffic.url LIKE "%/attachments/%" AND All_Traffic.app!="discord.exe" AND All_Traffic.app!="chrome.exe" AND All_Traffic.app!="msedge.exe" AND All_Traffic.app!="firefox.exe" by host All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.url All_Traffic.dest 
| `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` 
| rename host as dvc 
| join type=inner dvc [ 
  | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process="*\\\\.\\PhysicalDrive*" OR Processes.process="*\\\\?\\PhysicalDrive*" OR Processes.process="*CreateFile*PhysicalDrive*" OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND Processes.process IN ("*New-Object System.IO.FileStream*PhysicalDrive*","*[System.IO.File]::WriteAllBytes*PhysicalDrive*"))) by host Processes.user Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_hash 
  | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` 
  | rename host as dvc] 
| where firstTime <= _time AND lastTime >= relative_time(_time,"-30m") 
| eval reason="Discord CDN pull + raw PhysicalDrive write within 30m — WhisperGate stager pattern" 
| table firstTime, dvc, user, app, url, process_name, process, parent_process_name, process_path, reason
ucs.kql:
// Cadet Blizzard / Ember Bear WhisperGate chain: Discord CDN pull → raw \\.\PhysicalDrive write
let Lookback = 7d;
let Window = 30m;
// 1. Non-browser, non-Discord process pulling an attachment from Discord CDN
let DiscordPull =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any ("cdn.discordapp.com","media.discordapp.net")
    | where RemoteUrl has "/attachments/"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (
        "discord.exe","update.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe",
        "brave.exe","opera.exe","iexplore.exe","teams.exe","slack.exe"
      )
    | project DiscordTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              PullProcess=InitiatingProcessFileName,
              PullCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              PullSha=InitiatingProcessSHA256, RemoteUrl;
// 2. Raw PhysicalDrive write attempt (MBR overwrite primitive used by WhisperGate stage1)
let RawDiskTouch =
    union
      ( DeviceProcessEvents
        | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
        | where AccountName !endswith "$"
        | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\\.\PhysicalDrive", @"\\?\PhysicalDrive")
           or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
               and ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)PhysicalDrive\d.*(WriteAllBytes|FileStream|Write\()")
        | project DiskTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
                  WriterProcess=FileName, WriterCmd=ProcessCommandLine,
                  WriterSha=SHA256, ParentProcess=InitiatingProcessFileName ),
      ( DeviceFileEvents
        | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
        | where FileName has "PhysicalDrive" or FolderPath has "PhysicalDrive"
        | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("defrag.exe","diskpart.exe","chkdsk.exe","system")
        | project DiskTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
                  WriterProcess=InitiatingProcessFileName,
                  WriterCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
                  WriterSha=InitiatingProcessSHA256, ParentProcess=InitiatingProcessParentFileName );
DiscordPull
| join kind=inner RawDiskTouch on DeviceId
| where DiskTime between (DiscordTime .. DiscordTime + Window)
| extend DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', DiskTime, DiscordTime)
| project DiscordTime, DiskTime, DelayMin, DeviceName,
          PullProcess, PullSha, RemoteUrl,
          WriterProcess, WriterSha, WriterCmd, ParentProcess
| order by DiskTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Cadet Blizzard / Ember Bear WhisperGate chain (Sentinel via MDE connector + ASIM)
let Lookback = 7d;
let Window = 30m;
let DiscordPull =
    ImWebSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where Url has_any ("cdn.discordapp.com","media.discordapp.net") and Url has "/attachments/"
    | where ActingProcessName !in~ ("discord.exe","update.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","iexplore.exe","teams.exe","slack.exe")
    | project DiscordTime=TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              PullProcess=ActingProcessName, PullCmd=ActingProcessCommandLine, Url;
let RawDiskTouch =
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\\.\PhysicalDrive", @"\\?\PhysicalDrive")
       or (TargetProcessName has_any ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
           and TargetProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)PhysicalDrive\d.*(WriteAllBytes|FileStream|Write\()")
    | project DiskTime=TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              WriterProcess=TargetProcessName,
              WriterCmd=TargetProcessCommandLine,
              ParentProcess=ParentProcessName;
DiscordPull
| join kind=inner RawDiskTouch on DvcHostname
| where DiskTime between (DiscordTime .. DiscordTime + Window)
| extend DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', DiskTime, DiscordTime)
| project DiscordTime, DiskTime, DelayMin, DvcHostname,
          PullProcess, Url, WriterProcess, WriterCmd, ParentProcess
| order by DiskTime desc
ucs.rationale: Ember Bear's WhisperGate (Microsoft DEV-0586 / Cadet Blizzard) was the first publicly reported instance of a state actor abusing Discord's CDN as a wiper-payload host, and the stage1.exe wiper opens \\.\PhysicalDrive0 to overwrite the MBR — a primitive almost no benign Windows software uses outside diskpart/chkdsk. Joining the network half (cdn.discordapp.com/attachments from a non-browser, non-Di

ucs.title: [LLM] Ember Bear Impacket lateral movement — wmiexec/smbexec/atexec semaphore command line
ucs.description: Detects the canonical Impacket wmiexec.py / smbexec.py / atexec.py invocation fingerprint (`cmd.exe /Q /c <cmd> 1> \\127.0.0.1\ADMIN$\__<epoch> 2>&1`) which Ember Bear / UAC-0056 / Unit 29155 has repeatedly used for post-compromise lateral movement and credential collection on Ukrainian and European critical-infrastructure networks (CISA AA24-249A).
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="cmd.exe" Processes.process="*/Q*" Processes.process="*/c*" Processes.process="*2>&1*" (Processes.process="*\\\\127.0.0.1\\ADMIN$\\__*" OR Processes.process="*\\\\127.0.0.1\\C$\\__*" OR Processes.process IN ("*\\\\127.0.0.1\\ADMIN$\\*.bat","*\\\\127.0.0.1\\ADMIN$\\*.tmp")) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` 
| rex field=process "\\\\\\\\127\\.0\\.0\\.1\\\\(?<share>(ADMIN|C))\\$\\\\(?<output_file>\\S+)" 
| eval impacket_module=case(parent_process_name="WmiPrvSE.exe","wmiexec", parent_process_name="services.exe","smbexec", parent_process_name="taskeng.exe" OR parent_process_name="svchost.exe" AND match(parent_process,"(?i)Schedule"),"atexec", true(),"impacket_unknown") 
| where isnotnull(output_file) 
| table firstTime, host, user, impacket_module, parent_process_name, process, output_file
ucs.kql:
// Ember Bear (UAC-0056 / Unit 29155) Impacket wmiexec / smbexec / atexec fingerprint
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName =~ "cmd.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has "/Q" and ProcessCommandLine has "/c" and ProcessCommandLine has "2>&1"
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\\\\127\.0\.0\.1\\(ADMIN|C)\$\\(__\d{6,}\.\d+|[A-Za-z0-9]{6,}\.(bat|tmp|log))"
| extend ImpacketModule = case(
    InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "WmiPrvSE.exe", "wmiexec",
    InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "services.exe", "smbexec",
    (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "svchost.exe" and InitiatingProcessCommandLine has "Schedule") or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "taskeng.exe", "atexec",
    "impacket_unknown")
| extend OutputFile = extract(@"(?i)\\\\127\.0\.0\.1\\(?:ADMIN|C)\$\\(\S+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ImpacketModule,
          ParentProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ProcessCommandLine, OutputFile,
          IsRemote = IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Ember Bear (UAC-0056 / Unit 29155) Impacket wmiexec / smbexec / atexec fingerprint (Sentinel)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\cmd.exe"
| where CommandLine has "/Q" and CommandLine has "/c" and CommandLine has "2>&1"
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\\\\127\.0\.0\.1\\(ADMIN|C)\$\\(__\d{6,}\.\d+|[A-Za-z0-9]{6,}\.(bat|tmp|log))"
| extend ImpacketModule = case(
    ParentProcessName endswith "\\WmiPrvSE.exe", "wmiexec",
    ParentProcessName endswith "\\services.exe", "smbexec",
    ParentProcessName endswith "\\svchost.exe" or ParentProcessName endswith "\\taskeng.exe", "atexec",
    "impacket_unknown")
| extend OutputFile = extract(@"(?i)\\\\127\.0\.0\.1\\(?:ADMIN|C)\$\\(\S+)", 1, CommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ImpacketModule,
          ParentProcessName, CommandLine, OutputFile, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Impacket wmiexec/smbexec/atexec Lateral Execution (Ember Bear / UAC-0056 / Unit 29155)
id: 9b4d5a7e-d2c6-4e63-9c82-emberbear29155
status: experimental
description: Detects the cmd.exe semaphore output redirection pattern used by Impacket's wmiexec.py, smbexec.py and atexec.py ("cmd.exe /Q /c <cmd> 1> \\\\127.0.0.1\\ADMIN$\\__<epoch> 2>&1"). CISA AA24-249A and Microsoft attribute heavy use of Impacket lateral-movement tooling to Ember Bear (Cadet Blizzard / DEV-0586 / UAC-0056 / GRU Unit 29155) against Ukrainian government, telecom and Western critical-infrastructure targets.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1003/
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-249a
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2023/06/14/cadet-blizzard-emerges-as-a-novel-and-distinct-russian-threat-actor/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
  - attack.lateral_movement
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1021.002
  - attack.t1047
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.g1003
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_cmd:
    Image|endswith: '\cmd.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '/Q'
      - '/c'
      - '2>&1'
  selection_share:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '\\127.0.0.1\ADMIN$\__'
      - '\\127.0.0.1\C$\__'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_cmd and selection_share and not filter_machine
fields:
  - Image
  - ParentImage
  - CommandLine
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Rare administrative scripts that intentionally redirect output to admin shares
level: high
ucs.rationale: Both CISA's joint advisory AA24-249A on Unit 29155 and Microsoft's Cadet Blizzard reporting call out Ember Bear's heavy reliance on the Impacket suite (wmiexec, smbexec, atexec) for lateral movement and credential dumping — far more than typical e-crime crews who prefer PsExec or Cobalt Strike's jump-psexec. The `cmd.exe /Q /c ... 1> \\127.0.0.1\ADMIN$\__<epoch> 2>&1` semaphore is hard-coded in Im

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
